QF.id: XXVA Code of Conduct and Ethical Standards_3

question.text: The RA Code of Conduct explicitly prohibits research analysts from engaging in:

answer.text: Publishing research reports with sell recommendations
Insider trading or front-running or front-running of their own research report
Updating previously published research with new recommendations
Covering multiple companies within the same industry sector

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Code item 4 explicitly prohibits insider trading, front-running, and front-running of the analyst's own research report. These are zero-tolerance violations. **The Trap:** Sell recommendations, updates, and multi-company coverage are all normal research activities. The prohibitions are specifically on trading misconduct. **Cross-Exam Context:** Front-running is tested across Series XV, VIII, and III-A exams. Insider trading is covered in Series III-A and III-C. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Front-running your own entity's research report — even indirectly through tips to friends or family — is one of the most common ways sales personnel get into regulatory trouble. Don't do it.
